feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP GROW Fast: Швидкий старт для компаній у сфері професійних послуг
entry.content:
Компанії у сфері професійних послуг часто стикаються з однією і тією ж проблемою: розрізнені системи, ручне зведення даних між фінансами, проєктами та операціями — і жодної єдиної картини бізнесу.

Що таке SAP GROW Fast?

SAP GROW Fast — це повноцінний сервіс впровадження, який забезпечує перехід на SAP Cloud ERP і виводить компанію на повну операційну готовність за кілька тижнів з фіксованою вартістю та передбачуваним результатом. Рішення створено для консалтингових фірм, IT-компаній, агентств та інших організацій, де в центрі бізнесу — люди, проєкти та клієнти.

Рішення охоплює готові PSA-процеси (Professional Services Automation) — від першого етапу проєкту до виставлення рахунку: планування проєктів, управління ресурсами, фіксація часу та витрат, виставлення рахунків, фінансова аналітика в реальному часі та AI-аналітика. Все це — на єдиній платформі, з наскрізною прозорістю від старту проєкту до отримання оплати та повною локалізацією для України: відповідність законодавству, інтеграція з КЕП та україномовний інтерфейс.

Реальна історія успіху: Business Evolution

Business Evolution — українська IT-консалтингова компанія, яка здійснила перехід з 1С на SAP Cloud ERP за 90 днів і отримала єдину платформу для повноцінного управління клієнтськими проєктами. Цифри говорять самі за себе:

  • 60% скорочення часу фінансового закриття — до 4 днів
  • 80% автоматизація опрацювання клієнтських рахунків
  • 122 клієнтські проєкти об’єднані в одній фінансовій моделі
  • 30% скорочення витрат на персонал

🏆Проєкт здобув перемогу в регіоні CEE у SAP Quality Awards 2025 у категорії «Rapid Time to Value» — за швидкий запуск, високу якість реалізації та реальний бізнес-ефект.

Детальна інформація про SAP GROW Fast, повний опис рішення та умови — на лендінг-сторінці SAP GROW Fast.

Хочете дізнатись більше?

Щоб побачити рішення в дії та почути, як колеги по індустрії вже вирішують ці виклики — приєднуйтесь до вебінару! 
SAP Professional Services Webinar - 2 червня, 16:00–17:30 за українським часом.
Програма охопить ключові теми: огляд трендів індустрії, End-to-End ProServ процеси з SAP-рішеннями, AI у сфері професійних послуг — від теорії до практичної цінності, живе демо системи в дії, клієнтські кейси та детальний огляд SAP GROW Fast. Захід дасть вам можливість отримати практичні інсайти та визначити наступні кроки для зростання.

Не пропустіть — реєструйтесь за посиланням та додавайте до календаря! 

💬Поділіться цим постом з колегами у сфері професійних послуг — можливо, саме вони шукають відповідь на ці питання прямо зараз.

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026: What the announcements really mean for ECC and S/4HANA customers
entry.content:
The five key building blocks

1. Autonomous Enterprise: Humans set the direction, AI executes.

The Autonomous Enterprise is the overarching concept behind everything SAP is announcing this year. The idea: decisions are based on real-time data, workflows run end-to-end, and AI continuously improves operational processes. Crucially for regulated industries, SAP emphasizes fully governed AI – meaning AI that is transparent and controlled. Especially when it comes to financial, operational, and regulatory decisions, this isn't just a marketing ploy, but a prerequisite for using these functions productively at all.

2. Joule Work: One interface for the entire company

Joule Work combines AI assistants and agents in a single workspace. Instead of switching between applications, users define what they want to achieve – the system then coordinates the steps, gathers information, and initiates workflows.

From a user's perspective, this is probably the most tangible change: less "where can I find that?" and more "what do I want to do?". Whether this actually reduces friction in everyday use depends on how well the agents are integrated into the respective business processes – and on which SAP edition the customer is running (more on this below).

3. SAP Autonomous Suite: The operational core

Across finance, supply chain, procurement, HR, and customer experience, assistants and agents collaborate to manage entire processes—not just individual tasks. The shift from "automating individual steps" to "end-to-end execution" is conceptually crucial.

In practice, this shifts routine work. Employees focus on decisions and exceptions instead of manually coordinating workflows.

4. Industry AI: Solutions that understand the industry context

Generic AI functions are often insufficient in regulated or highly specialized industries. With Industry AI , SAP combines applications, data, and AI building blocks to meet the specific requirements of individual industries.

For customers in pharmaceuticals, automotive, banking, utilities or the public sector, this is likely to be more relevant than broad cross-functional areas – provided that the respective industry solution is actually available and not just announced.

5. SAP Business AI Platform: The basis

The platform brings together business data, applications, and AI services in a single environment. It addresses a problem that most companies are familiar with from their own pilot projects: Getting a single AI use case up and running is feasible; operating it consistently, securely, and scalably is significantly more difficult. The platform is the prerequisite for transforming individual use cases into a viable operating model.

The honest question is: How much of this actually reaches ECC customers?

One must be realistic here. A large part of the announced AI functions – especially Joule Work, the end-to-end agent orchestration of the Autonomous Suite, and many Industry AI building blocks – are based on the modern SAP platform, usually S/4HANA Cloud (Public or Private Edition).

For ECC customers, this means specifically:

  • Mainstream maintenance for ECC 6.0 ends at the end of 2027; extended maintenance is available for an additional fee until the end of 2030.
  • The more ambitious AI promises cannot be implemented on ECC, or only to a very limited extent.
  • The Sapphire announcements increase the pressure to migrate to S/4HANA – but they do not change the known timeline.

This is no surprise, but rather the logical continuation of SAP's established strategy. Those still running on ECC should view these announcements less as "new features for me" and more as an additional reason to finalize their migration plans.

And what about S/4HANA customers?

Here too, a distinction is worthwhile, because not all S/4HANA is the same:

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition will receive the new AI features the fastest and most completely.
  • S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition / RISE with SAP receives a large portion of the functions, depending on the release level and the chosen service model.
  • S/4HANA On-Premise traditionally has the least and slowest access to new AI features. Those who remain with this system should realistically assess which of Sapphire's promises will actually be relevant – and which will not.

For many customers, this reignites the debate about the right S/4 edition. The question of "cloud or on-premise?" increasingly determines the pace of innovation one can achieve.

Perhaps the most practical part: Agent-led Transformation

Perhaps the most important announcement from a customer perspective comes almost as an aside: SAP is using AI not only in operations, but also in the transformation itself. Joule Agents and Assistants are intended to automate tasks such as system analysis, data preparation, and test support.

This is particularly relevant for ECC customers facing an S/4 migration. Migrations rarely fail due to technical issues alone, but rather due to the effort required for analysis, cleansing, customization evaluation, and testing. If agent-led transformation measurably reduces these effort items, it's a practical lever – and not just a marketing ploy.

The important thing remains: Each customer should assess for their specific situation how much of this is already productively usable today and how much is merely a roadmap. A proof of concept on a real subsystem is more informative than any slideshow.

Conclusion

SAP Sapphire 2026 doesn't mark a break, but rather a consolidation of the strategy of recent years: AI becomes an operational component of the suite, not an optional add-on. The direction is clear, and the benefits for customers on modern editions are plausible.

For the majority of existing SAP customers – with ECC or older S/4 releases – the most important message is less technical than planning-related: The gap between "I'll stay on my current environment" and "I can use the new features" is widening. Agent-led transformation is intended to close this gap; whether it succeeds in practice will become clear in concrete projects over the coming months.

Anyone looking for a complete overview of all announcements can find it in the SAP Sapphire Innovation News Guide 2026 .

entry.title: SAP User Research Panel — допоможіть формувати майбутнє SAP
entry.content:
Чи замислювались ви, як SAP створює продукти, якими зручно користуватись? Відповідь проста: завдяки реальним людям — таким як ви.

Що таке SAP User Research Panel?

SAP запрошує співробітників та користувачів долучитись до ініціативи SAP User Research Panel. Це можливість безпосередньо впливати на те, як розвиваються продукти та рішення SAP. Після реєстрації ви отримуватимете запрошення на короткі дослідження у зручному для вас форматі — 30–60 хвилин, повністю віддалено: usability tests, surveys або interviews.

Чому це важливо?

User research — це не просто збір відгуків. Це інструмент, який допомагає продуктовим командам SAP глибше зрозуміти потреби користувачів і на основі реальних інсайтів приймати рішення щодо дизайну, розробки та планування продуктів. Результат — більш зручні, ефективні та корисні рішення для всіх.

Один з прикладів: компанія K+S — провідний виробник солі в Європі — завдяки user research та SAP Fiori суттєво підвищила ефективність своїх ремонтних команд на підземному руднику площею 61 км². Раніше через нестабільне мережеве з’єднання команди щодня друкували понад 1 000 паперових документів. Саме завдяки залученню кінцевих користувачів до процесу розробки — через спостереження, інтерв’ю та usability тести — вдалося створити рішення, яке дійсно відповідає реальним потребам команди.

"Раніше я як користувач мав адаптуватися до системи. Тепер система адаптується до користувача."
— Маріо Лоренц, K+S

Дізнатися більше про історію К+S

Як взяти участь?

Все просто — лише три кроки:

  1. Зареєструйтесь — вкажіть свої дані, щоб для вас підбирали релевантні дослідження
  2. Отримайте запрошення — коли з'явиться дослідження, що відповідає вашому профілю, ви отримаєте invite. Участь — виключно за вашим бажанням
  3. Долучайтесь віддалено — surveys можна пройти у зручний для вас час, usability testing зазвичай займає 30–60 хвилин

🖥 На даний момент панель орієнтована на колег з розробки та інженерії — якщо це про вас, це ваша можливість безпосередньо впливати на розвиток продуктів SAP.

Дізнатись більше: https://url.sap/jxjl38

Приєднатись: https://url.sap/9b0boo

Ваш досвід має значення — допоможіть SAP створювати продукти, які дійсно працюють для людей. 💡

entry.title: Example for Blog Refresher Session
entry.content:
• Introduction
  • Key Aspects
  • Manage Your Content
  • Have You Noticed?
  • Summary

Introduction

This blog aims to serve as an example for demonstrating how to create a blog post in the SAP Community platform.

Key Aspects

A meaningful title

A meaningful title enables audience to quickly grasp what the blog is about and whether it's relevant to them.

Apply the right structure

Every blog post is expected to consist of a beginning (introduction), a middle (main portion), and an end (a conclusion that wraps everything up). A collection of links or just a video with short text does not qualify as a blog post.

SAP Managed Tags

SAP Managed Tags are a type of tag derived from SAP's official list of products and solutions.

You can subscribe to an SAP managed tag so that you will receive notifications of new content about that product. 

You must add a tag to your content to indicate the product that is relevant for your blog.

All SAP Managed Tags | SAP Community

Blog Labels

Labels are used within the SAP Community to help categorize content in discussions, blogs, Q&As, and knowledge bases
Labels enable you to categorize the content you write based on the themes or content in the article. 

Unlike tags, labels are created by the Admin and typically controlled for consistency. Some Groups have a pre-defined list of labels, depending on the purpose and setup of the Group. In which case authors can choose/apply labels from the pre-defined list in the Group in which the article appears. Some Groups allow members to add their own labels. It is also possible to subscribe to a label to get notifications of new content associated with this label. 

Article User Tags

Article user tags are optional. They are used to further describe the topic or subject of the content, complementing the SAP Managed Tag. You can create your own user tags. For example, in a blog post about SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you might add these Article User Tags: implementation, guidance. Be sure to use commas between tags.

Cover Photos

Cover photos are optional. They are used only when a group or board owner decides to feature a blog post. But if you want to include a cover photo, the required dimensions are 600*420 pixels, as that size is the requirement for featured posts.

Scheduling for Publication

Select the date and time at the correct time zone to schedule your post for publication. This is helpful when you want to publish the content on a specific date when you're out of office.

Published link

You may have noticed that the format of a draft link is different from that of a published link. If you want to share the link to your colleague before you publish it, you should copy the published link.

Co-authors

The co-author functionality allows for other community members to assist during the editing and publishing process. Co-authors must be a member of the Community. You add a co-author by searching their user names.  

Tip: Consider adding a co-author if you have plans to go out of office, as this might impact the editing or publishing of the blog. It can also be helpful if you anticipate changes to your role or position (as has happened in practice). 

Note: The co-authors do not show in the user interface of the published post.
If you want to mention co-authors please do so as part of the post content, for instance thanking them at the end of the post.

Send Content for Review/Moderation

Posts from new bloggers will need to go through moderation before they are published. To bypass moderation, members will need to move up the rank ladder (although posts submitted to a group will always need to go through moderation). You can learn more about the rank ladder in the What's New post Let the Gamification Begin! If your post needs to go through moderation, please note that the moderation team may require up to five business days to conduct each review.

Recommendation: According to my own experience, the moderators usually publish your post sent for review directly if there are no further changes required. So ensure your content is really ready for publication before you send it for review.

Manage Your Content

You can find all your content in the "My Content" section.

Have You Noticed?

Table of Contents Button is Available

Allows you to create a table of contents, which provides audience with an overview of the blog content at the beginning of the blog.

Separate Buttons for Video Upload

Web video

Payment Inquiry Handling Agent - Demo video.mp4

SAP Media Share Video

 

Summary

In summary, this blog provides an overview of the key points of writing a blog post in the tool, and highlights the new features provided by the tool.

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: SAP Sapphire 2026 Special Edition
entry.content:
This week's edition of the SAP Community Voice celebrates one of our most waited and biggest events: SAP Sapphire 2026!  

The event that happened in Orlando and now in Madrid, gave the opportunity for attendees to learn about Joule’s new central workspace, experience live demos and see how Joule proactively improves operations. The highlights for our SAP community was also to connect with experts, partners, and peers to talk about AI strategies and insights about SAP new announcements.  

If you had the chance to be there in person or watch the keynotes virtually, you probably are excited about the new SAP Business AI Platform, which forms the basis for the future of business: the Autonomous Enterprise, where agents run the business and you can focus on what matters.  

And our team believes there’s no better place to start than by investing time in learning. Today, we are excited to share more details about some of the recent announcements that could help you reach the next level.

Check out now:

  • SAP Certification Turns 30 and Expands Its Portfolio for the AI Era:  

A transformed certification approach and new AI-assisted learning capabilities in SAP Learning Hub redefine how SAP professionals build, prove, and maintain their skills as SAP Certification turns 30 in 2026, marking both a milestone and the completion of the most significant transformation in the program's history. Building on the new performance-based assessment approach introduced at SAP TechEd 2025, the full SAP Certification portfolio of more than 100 certifications now moves away from traditional multiple-choice formats to hands-on practical assessments, while candidates can leverage AI tools during exams, mirroring the tools and resources they use every day. This is part of SAP's commitment to equipping 12 million people with AI-ready skills by 2030), building on the more than 4 million people already reached in 2025. 

 

  • Practical Assessments Across the Full Portfolio 

The assessment framework includes role-plays, challenge-based case studies, and practical tasks that mirror actual scenarios professionals encounter in SAP implementations, while system-based exams have Joule for Consultants built in and candidates can use AI support and documentation throughout, mirroring the tools and resources they rely on every day. Each certification includes comprehensive learning journeys and practical exercises, and more than 100,000 certification exams have already been taken under the new model. According to CIO magazine, SAP is among the first major enterprise software vendors to implement this shift at scale.  

Available globally now at https://learning.sap.com/get-certified/reimagining-certification.

 

  • SAP Learning Hub Introduces AI-Assisted Learning Capabilities 

Alongside the certification transformation, SAP Learning Hub, the single destination for customers and partners to learn, practice, and prove their SAP skills, is introducing new AI-assisted capabilities in its customer and partner editions. Already embraced by over 7,500 early users, the new capabilities allow learners to engage with SAP course content beyond traditional linear learning by asking questions and getting instant answers in over 80 languages, with precise citations from SAP's trusted learning content, while additional AI-generated formats include podcasts, interactive sessions with AI hosts, automatically generated FAQs, mind maps, study guides, quizzes, and video overviews. The business impact is measurable: LTIMindtree reported 50 percent faster onboarding, and for Dieter Schoon, Chief People Officer at NTT Data Business Solutions, SAP Learning Hub has become the primary way their talent becomes future-ready in the agentic AI era. 

 Available globally now at https://learning.sap.com/learninghub-subscriptions.

 

  • SAP introduces new learning journey, certification for data architects 

As AI becomes embedded across enterprise operations, the quality of the data it relies on can be a competitive differentiator. SAP is introducing “Becoming a Certified SAP Data Architect,” a new learning journey that includes a certification in SAP Business Data Cloud, designed to help data architects build the data foundation AI requires. The learning journey addresses a key challenge for enterprise AI (data fragmented across disconnected systems) by helping learners develop skills to connect, manage, and govern data across the enterprise using SAP Business Data Cloud, ensuring AI models can access consistent and reliable information while enabling teams to move faster from raw data to business decisions. Data architects who complete the learning journey and earn the certification will be prepared to support a data fabric approach, so data stays coherent and accessible across the enterprise. The learning journey is now available on the SAP Learning site.

 

  • SAP Expands Access to AI-Ready Skills with New Learning Offerings and Accenture Partnership 

As the AI-ready skills gap becomes a defining challenge for organizations worldwide, with the World Economic Forum identifying skills shortages as the primary barrier to business transformation ahead of investment constraints and regulatory complexity, SAP is expanding its learning portfolio with new offerings designed to increase access, relevance, and scalability of AI-ready skills development, supporting its commitment to equip 12 million people with AI-ready skills by 2030 (Read more details here).

 

  • Accenture Becomes First Global Authorized Partner to Deliver SAP Learning Class 

SAP is introducing a partner-led delivery model for SAP Learning Class, its premium instructor-led training offering, with Accenture becoming the first SAP Authorized Education Partner and SAP Global Strategic Services Partner to deliver these courses worldwide. The model combines SAP-authored content and pre-configured SAP training systems with Accenture's global delivery capabilities, industry expertise, and local accessibility across time zones and geographies, while Accenture instructors bring practical insight into how SAP integrates within complete enterprise technology stacks, including SAP's AI capabilities. SAP Learning Class can also be combined with self-paced learning on the SAP Learning site, creating a continuous development path from foundational knowledge to hands-on experience and SAP Certification. 

Available globally now at SAP Training Shop.

 

  • SAP Makes Selected AI Live Sessions Available to All Learners 

SAP is offering free AI-focused live sessions open to everyone, covering Business AI topics across awareness, adoption, and demonstrations: 

  • Accelerate your transformation to the Cloud with SAP Business AI 
  • Efficient Document Inbound Processing in SAP Cloud ERP with SAP Document AI 
  • All Things Business AI: Latest Insights, Updates & Live Demos 
  • Build AI Securely: Avoid Pitfalls in the Development and Operations Lifecycle 

Available now at https://learning.sap.com/artificial-intelligence.

 

  • New Learning Journey for Enterprise Architects Leading AI Transformation 

Enterprise architects play a central role in shaping AI adoption and transformation strategies, yet role-specific training for this audience has been largely absent from the market. The new learning journey, “Leading AI-Driven Transformation as an SAP Enterprise Architect,” addresses this gap by providing frameworks and guidance on aligning AI initiatives with business objectives, assessing organizational readiness, managing architectural risk, and designing scalable, secure enterprise landscapes. 

Available globally from May 2026, free of charge at SAP Learning site.

 

  • Clean Core: Building the Foundation for Innovation and AI Readiness 

A standardized and upgrade-stable SAP S/4HANA core enables organizations to adopt new capabilities more quickly, reduce upgrade cycles from months to days, and free resources for innovation, while also creating the data foundation AI requires through standardized data models and reduced system fragmentation that allow AI solutions to access consistent, enterprise-wide data. The course “Clean Core: Applying Clean Core Principles in SAP S/4HANA” provides guidance on governance, architecture, and cloud-compliant extensions for developers, consultants, and architects responsible for building future-ready SAP environments. 

Available globally now, free of charge 2026 at SAP Learning site 

For more details, please check the SAP Sapphire Innovation News Guide 2026  

SAP Community at SAP Sapphire 2026  

Beyond the amazing news, for our SAP community the event also becomes a wonderful opportunity to get insight from the experts, network with professionals from all over the world, learn more details about all sorts of SAP topics and meet other members. This year we were very proud to have the SAP Mentors and the SAP Champions (@david_ruizb , @Dominik_Tylczynski , @tonjakehrer, @sumikko) in the event. In case you also attended the event in Orlando, Madrid or even virtually, we would like to encourage you to write a blog post telling your opinions, insights and thoughts about the event. You can share the content inside the Technology Blog Post, in the Community Corner, or any other group or topic of your preference (check here the list of all interest groups). The idea is to give you an open space to share what you think about SAP new vision for how business will run from now on.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Basel–Freiburg: Austausch trotz Ferienzeit und Eishockey-WM
Sydney Stammtisch Recap May '26
entry.content:
We had a great time up on The Rooftop at the Shelbourne, despite another showery day.  This was the view as I climbed the stairs

We had an excellend turnout with a good mix of first-timers, second- or third-timers and longstanding regulars. With the roof closed it did get very noisy and my voice was hoarse by the end of the evening. Thanks to everyone that came, especially those who brought a friend or colleague.

The next event will be on Thursday 6th August so keep it free!  We will try and find a quieter venue....

If you have a venue in mind for our next event, why not add a comment below? You can add your own photos too

SAPStammtischSYD is a proud to be an 'SAP Community' event.  It isn't an official SAP event and we aren't managed by SAP employees

entry.title: Recife em Grande Estilo: Inovação, IA e a Força Inabalável da Comunidade no SAP Inside Track
entry.content:
O último sábado foi marcado por um dos momentos mais vibrantes do ano para o ecossistema de tecnologia do Nordeste. Recife respirou inovação, partilha de conhecimento e colaboração com a realização de mais uma edição do SAP Inside Track (SIT) Recife. O evento consolidou-se, de forma indiscutível, como uma das principais referências nacionais para a comunidade SAP, combinando uma organização minuciosa e de excelência com conteúdos que estão na vanguarda do mercado global.

O sucesso foi tanto que o reflexo mais evidente estava no próprio espaço físico: uma sala absolutamente cheia, com profissionais entusiasmados espremendo-se para absorver cada minuto de conteúdo. O diagnóstico para o próximo ano já está dado — o SIT Recife cresceu tanto que será estritamente necessário encontrar um novo espaço, ainda maior, para comportar o tamanho e a energia desse ecossistema em expansão.

A Nova Era da Inteligência e do Business AI

Como não poderia deixar de ser, a Inteligência Artificial foi a grande protagonista dos debates do dia. O grande assunto que dominou os corredores e painéis foi a transição e evolução do SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), agora renomeado para BAIP (Business AI Platform). Essa mudança estratégica reflete o peso que a IA ganhou no core das soluções da companhia.

Muito além dos conceitos teóricos, as sessões demonstraram de forma prática como as ferramentas de IA embarcadas já estão prontas para o ambiente corporativo, atuando diretamente na simplificação de processos e tornando-se o grande diferencial competitivo para os negócios modernos. A IA deixou de ser uma promessa de futuro para se tornar uma realidade operacional indispensável.

Destaques Técnicos: Casos Reais que Inspiram

Entre a rica grade de apresentações, algumas sessões merecem um destaque muito especial pela profundidade técnica e capacidade de entrega:

  • O Case do MES (Manufacturing Execution System) : Apresentado de forma magistral pelo Caio Filizola e pelo Michel Filipe, o caso prático trouxe uma visão cirúrgica e realista da Indústria 4.0. Eles demonstraram com maestria como a integração do ecossistema de manufatura pode transformar o chão de fábrica, otimizando a produtividade e gerando eficiência operacional real através das soluções SAP. Uma verdadeira aula de execução desenvolvida pela Deloitte.
  • Excelência em Processos com SAP Signavio: A condução do Rogério Toscano sobre o SAP Signavio foi outro ponto alto do evento. Com uma clareza excepcional, Rogério ilustrou como a plataforma se tornou o pilar central para a transformação de processos corporativos. A apresentação detalhou a modelagem, análise e otimização de operações de ponta a ponta, provando o valor estratégico que a inteligência de processos agrega à governança corporativa moderna.

Liderança e Dedicação à Comunidade

Por trás de um evento desse porte e de uma atmosfera tão rica, existe um esforço humano monumental que merece ser reverenciado. Mais uma vez, Recife demonstrou o seu papel de liderança e protagonismo na comunidade SAP brasileira.

Um agradecimento e referência especial devem ser feitos ao Marssel Vilaça e à Roberta Fernandes. Com uma dedicação impecável e um esforço pessoal incancável, eles são os verdadeiros guardiões que mantêm viva a chama das comunidades SAP no Brasil, conectando pessoas e impulsionando o desenvolvimento profissional de toda a região.

O SAP Inside Track Recife deste ano não entregou apenas conteúdo técnico de altíssimo nível, mas reforçou o valor insubstituível do networking presencial, da paixão pelo ecossistema e do poder de transformação de uma comunidade unida. Parabéns a todos os envolvidos!

#SAPInsideTrack #SITRecife #ComunidadeSAP #SAPBAIP #SAPSignavio #MES #InovacaoTecnologica #InteligenciaArtificial #Networking

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2026: The Autonomous Enterprise Has Arrived
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire 2026: The Autonomous Enterprise Has Arrived

A complete summary of the biggest AI announcements from Orlando, May 13–14, 2026

50+

Joule Assistants

200+

Specialized Agents

€100M

Partner AI Fund

26

Industries

SAP Sapphire 2026 in Orlando marked what many are calling the most significant shift in enterprise software in decades. CEO Christian Klein unveiled the Autonomous Enterprise — a bold repositioning of SAP from 'software that records what people do' to 'software that does the work itself.' With over 50 Joule Assistants and 200+ specialized AI agents shipping across Finance, Spend Management, Supply Chain, HR, and Customer Experience, SAP is making a clear statement: the era of autonomous business operations is here.

This blog post covers all 12 major announcement areas from the event, based on official SAP News Center announcements.

1. The Autonomous Enterprise — Christian Klein's Vision

"Eighty percent accuracy isn't sufficient when running mission-critical businesses. AI must work at enterprise grade — accurate, compliant, and secure."  — Christian Klein, CEO, SAP

The keynote set the tone for the entire conference. SAP's strategic pivot centers on three pillars:

  • SAP Business AI Platform — A unified build-and-govern layer combining BTP, Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation with SAP Knowledge Graph at its core
  • SAP Autonomous Suite — 200+ specialized agents and 50+ Joule Assistants across all major business functions, shipping now
  • Joule Work — A completely new human interface replacing traditional app navigation, described as the most significant UX shift since Fiori

A live on-stage demo with H&M showcased Store Intelligence Agents and an AI-powered InStore Concierge delivering personalized product recommendations — a compelling real-world proof point of the vision.

2. SAP Business AI Platform — One Foundation

BTP, Business Data Cloud, and AI Foundation have been unified into a single architecture, with the SAP Knowledge Graph at the core:

01

SAP Knowledge Graph: 452,000 SAP tables encoded; 7.3 million data fields with semantic relationships; 50 years of SAP ERP engineering

02

Anthropic Claude: Named primary AI reasoning model powering Joule — multi-step reasoning for enterprise-grade workflows with full security and compliance controls

03

Joule Studio 2.0 (GA Now): AI-first dev environment integrating n8n visual workflow, Vercel design tooling, and NVIDIA OpenShell secure runtime

04

SAP AI Agent Hub (Q3 2026): Central marketplace for discovering, managing, and governing AI agents — 680+ partner submissions already received

05

Prior Labs / SAP-RPT-1.5: Tops TabArena benchmark — 3.5x better than LLMs on structured enterprise predictions

06

Dremio (Q3 2026): Open Lakehouse acquisition extending Business Data Cloud with open-format analytics at scale

07

Microsoft Fabric Connect (GA): SAP data flows natively into Microsoft Fabric without duplication — zero-copy integration

08

AWS Zero-Copy (GA): SAP data accessible in Amazon Athena — no data movement required

3. Joule Work — The New Human Interface

Joule Work abandons traditional app navigation entirely. Describe outcomes in natural language and Joule coordinates data, workflows, and agents to execute. This is the biggest UX shift since Fiori.

Availability

  • Mobile App — Available Now: Full Joule Work experience on mobile devices
  • Web Client — Early Adopter Care: GA targeted for H2 2026
  • Desktop App — Planned Q2 2026: Bridges SAP with local files, email, and documents
  • Voice via LiveKit — Early Adopter Care: Real-time voice AI for field, factory, and warehouse

Interoperability Protocols

  • Model Context Protocol (MCP) — Available Now: Joule connects natively to Microsoft Copilot and third-party agents
  • Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Protocol — GA Q4 2026: Bidirectional integration with Salesforce Agentforce and ServiceNow

4. Autonomous Finance — 7 Joule Assistants (GA Q3 2026)

"72% of organizations find financial planning too time-consuming."  — SAP Research

Finance operations follow a Sense–Reason–Act model: detect signals, evaluate constraints in real time, connect plans to execution — automatically. The 7 Joule Assistants cover the full financial lifecycle:

01

Financial Closing Assistant: Compresses close cycle from weeks to days

02

Financial Planning Assistant: Continuous planning with SAP Analytics Cloud

03

Billing Assistant: Detects inconsistencies before invoice generation

04

Governance Assistant: CSRD and regulatory compliance tracking

05

Tax & Compliance Assistant: BEPS Pillar 2 and global e-invoicing mandates

06

Accounts Receivable Assistant: Churn prediction and automated dunning

07

Cash & Treasury Assistant: Real-time liquidity visibility and allocation

SAP Enterprise Planning is the new flagship product — moving finance from periodic planning cycles to continuous steering, with Joule Agents embedded to monitor signals and recommend actions automatically.

5. Autonomous Spend Management — 11 Joule Assistants

The full source-to-pay lifecycle is now covered by 11 Joule Assistants, addressing long-standing fragmentation between procurement, finance, and operations. Key milestones include Ariba Buying reaching GA and Fieldglass introducing AI-enhanced contractor management. Assistants span Category Management, Sourcing, Contract Management, Supplier Collaboration, Purchase Requisitions, Invoice Processing, Expense Management, Travel Management, Workforce Planning, Contingent Workforce, and Spend Analytics.

6. Autonomous Supply Chain — 6 Joule Assistants + 60+ Agents

Supply chain sees 6 Joule Assistants alongside 60+ specialized agents covering demand planning, production scheduling, logistics, and asset management. Notable highlights:

  • NVIDIA Partnership — Factory-edge AI for sub-second quality inspection and anomaly detection
  • RWE Demo — Offshore wind turbine maintenance using autonomous asset management agents
  • Assistants cover: Demand Planning, Production Scheduling, Logistics, Inventory Management, Supplier Risk, and Asset Maintenance

7. Autonomous HCM — 13 Joule Assistants (All GA June 2026)

Human Capital Management gets the largest single-function rollout — 13 Joule Assistants all reaching General Availability in June 2026. The suite covers the complete employee lifecycle from recruiting through retirement, with notable integrations:

  • SmartRecruiters Integration — AI-enhanced talent acquisition workflows
  • Workforce Planning — Predictive headcount and skills gap analysis
  • Full coverage: Recruiting, Onboarding, Learning, Performance, Succession, Compensation, Payroll, Benefits, Time & Attendance, Employee Experience, HR Service Delivery, Analytics, and Workforce Planning

8. Autonomous Customer Experience — 11 Joule Assistants

The CX suite is built on the principle that every customer interaction reflects how well the business runs — AI must operate across the full execution chain. 11 Joule Assistants cover marketing through service:

  • Content, Campaign, Merchandising, Shopping, Order Management
  • Sales, Deal Qualification, Deal Closing
  • Case Management, Service Management, Content Generation

Key CX Updates

  • SAP Commerce Cloud for Mid-Market — GA Q3 2026
  • Composable Commerce Services — New modular cart and checkout
  • Google Cloud — Gemini Enterprise + Universal Commerce Protocol for multimodal personalized journeys
  • Parloa — Voice and digital self-service in SAP Service Cloud
  • Adyen / Checkout.com / PayPal — Embedded payment processing in SAP Commerce workflows

9. Migration & Modernisation — 35%+ Effort Reduction

 

35%+

ERP Migration Effort Reduction

3

Joule Assistants in RISE Year 1

3

S/4HANA Release Cycles Certified

 

  • RISE with SAP Restructured — Now activates 3 Joule Assistants contractually in Year 1; ECC customers can access select AI scenarios immediately before full migration completes
  • SAP GROW — Full Joule portfolio access from day one for mid-market cloud-first customers
  • Clean Core Certification — Formally certifies BTP extensions as upgrade-compatible across three S/4HANA Cloud release cycles
  • Palantir AIP Partnership (GA Q3 2026) — SAP Endorsed App status with Accenture as global strategic services partner

 

10. Strategic Partnerships Reshaping the Ecosystem

SAP announced 11 strategic alliances spanning AI reasoning, infrastructure, data, experience, and interoperability:

 

01

Anthropic: Claude named primary reasoning model powering Joule across the full Autonomous Suite

02

NVIDIA: OpenShell runtime for Joule Studio; factory-edge inference for sub-second quality inspection

03

Microsoft: Fabric Connect GA; A2A interoperability with Microsoft Copilot (Q4 2026)

04

Google Cloud: Gemini Enterprise for CX; Universal Commerce Protocol; Merchant Center integration

05

AWS: Zero-copy SAP data access via Amazon Athena — no data movement required

06

Palantir: AIP as SAP Endorsed App + Solution Extension; GA Q3 2026 with Accenture as global partner

07

Accenture: Global strategic services partner for Palantir AIP and complex S/4 migrations

08

n8n: Visual workflow builder integrated into Joule Studio for no-code agent design

09

Vercel: Design tooling in Joule Studio; accelerates storefront development for CX

10

LiveKit: Real-time voice AI for Joule Work in field, factory, and warehouse settings

11

Parloa: AI agents in SAP Service Cloud for end-to-end voice and digital service resolution

 

11. Partner Ecosystem — €100M Business AI Fund

"A Symphony of Partnership to Ring in the Era of the Autonomous Enterprise."  — Karl Fahrbach, Chief Partner Officer, SAP

The €100M Business AI Partner-Led Adoption Program is structured around four service packages:

  • Adoption — Entry level: deploy existing AI assistants with structured enablement
  • Launch — Build and deploy at least one custom agent via Joule Studio
  • Performance — Demonstrate measurable business outcomes from deployed agents
  • Enterprise — Comprehensive AI strategy delivery with full Joule Studio mastery

 

Additional milestones include a new Validated Partner Designation for AI and data strategy expertise, the Partner Agent Race to SAP TechEd with 680+ submissions, and a full enablement curriculum across sales, presales, consulting, and development roles.

 

12. Industry AI — 26 Sectors at GA

SAP is embedding domain logic directly into the Autonomous Suite for 26 industries. Seven sectors are live at launch:

  • Utilities — Autonomous asset management; RWE offshore wind turbine maintenance demo
  • Manufacturing — Factory-edge AI with NVIDIA; quality inspection and production excellence agents
  • Life Sciences — Compliance-aware manufacturing and regulatory documentation for pharma
  • Consumer & Retail — Unified commerce, revenue growth management, AI-powered in-store concierge (H&M live keynote)
  • Oil, Gas & Chemicals — Asset-intensive scenarios via Fieldglass; contractor lifecycle and credential verification
  • Engineering & Construction — Project and resource management with supply chain integration
  • + 20 More Industries — Agriculture, Banking, Insurance, and 17 others at full GA

 

Key Takeaways for the SAP Community

SAP Sapphire 2026 wasn't a roadmap event — it was a delivery event. The capabilities announced are shipping now or within the next two quarters. Here's what the SAP community needs to know:

 

  1. Joule Work changes how users interact with SAP

The shift from menu-driven navigation to natural language is fundamental. Partners and customers should begin planning adoption strategies now.

  1. Clean Core is now certifiable and incentivized

With Clean Core Certification and the RISE restructuring, there's never been a clearer path for ECC customers to modernize without losing access to AI capabilities during migration.

  1. The partner opportunity is enormous

The €100M fund, 680+ agent submissions, and four-tier adoption program create a massive opportunity for SAP partners to build, deploy, and monetize AI capabilities.

  1. Industry AI is real and customer-validated

H&M and RWE weren't just demos — they were production-grade live deployments on stage. This signals that enterprise-grade autonomous operations are already in production for SAP's largest customers.

Based on official SAP News Center announcements from SAP Sapphire 2026, Orlando, May 13–14, 2026.

For more information, visit community.sap.com

entry.title: Autonomous Enterprise: SAP змінює правила гри — і допомагає вам підготуватися
entry.content:
На конференції SAP Sapphire 2026 компанія SAP офіційно оголосила про старт концепції Autonomous Enterprise (Автономне підприємство)— фундаментальну зміну того, як проектуються, управляються та функціонують компанії в епоху ШІ.

З чого складається Autonomous Enterprise?

  • SAP Business AI Platform об’єднує BTP, SAP Business Data Cloud та SAP Business AI в єдине середовище. В основі — SAP Knowledge Graph та Joule Studio для створення агентів у форматі no-code, pro-code або AI-фреймворків.
  • SAP Autonomous Suite включає 50+ асистентів Joule та 200+ агентів для автоматизації наскрізних процесів. Наприклад, Autonomous Close Assistant може скоротити фінансове закриття з кількох тижнів до кількох днів.
  • Joule Work — єдина точка взаємодії замість десятків застосунків. Користувач описує бажаний результат, а Joule координує процеси, дані та агентів. Доступно на десктопі, мобільних пристроях та через голосовий інтерфейс.

Інвестиція у 100 млн євро

SAP запускає фонд для прискорення переходу до Autonomous Enterprise:

  • Клієнти RISE with SAP — три активовані асистенти Joule в перший рік
  • Клієнти SAP GROW — повний доступ до портфоліо з першого дня
  • Нові інструменти міграції скорочують зусилля на перехід до хмарного ERP більш ніж на 35%

Безкоштовна серія вебінарів

На тлі оголошень із Sapphire SAP запускає безкоштовну серію вебінарів «Architecting the Autonomous Enterprise» — 11 сесій, розроблених архітекторами для архітекторів.

📅 1 червня — 12 серпня 2026, щотижня
🕔 18:00–19:00 за українським часом

Кожна сесія ведеться практиками, лідерами продуктів, клієнтами та запрошеними спікерами, які самі будують, управляють або консультують щодо тих самих архітектурних рішень. Формат — одна сфокусована тема на сесію з повноцінним Q&A.

Програма сесій:

Тема

Дата

Unpacking SAP’s Autonomous Enterprise for Architects

1 червня 2026

What SAP’s Evolved Portfolio Means for Architects

10 червня 2026

Architecting From Intent to Action with Joule Work & Knowledge Graph

16 червня 2026

Playbook for Building Your Own SAP Agents (Hands-On)

26 червня 2026

SAP’s Innovation Factory for Architecting and Activating AI

1 липня 2026

AI Agent Lifecycle Management at Enterprise Scale

8 липня 2026

An Architect’s Guide to the SAP Family of Models

14 липня 2026

Data Foundations for an Autonomous Enterprise

22 липня 2026

From Autonomous Domains to Industry AI: One Platform, Two Lenses

29 липня 2026

AI Governance and Security for an Autonomous Enterprise

5 серпня 2026

Role of an Enterprise Architect in Driving the Autonomous Enterprise

12 серпня 2026

* To be Finalised 
детальна інформація про спікерів та розклад - за посиланням

Для кого ця серія?
Для Enterprise, Solution, Data та AI Architects, Technology Leaders, а також SAP клієнтів і партнерів, що впроваджують або масштабують ШІ в корпоративних середовищах.

Що ви отримаєте?

  • Цілісну архітектурну модель Joule Work, SAP Autonomous Suite, Business AI Platform та Knowledge Graph
  •  Практичне керівництво з побудови та управління SAP-агентами
  •  Реальні кейси від Bayer та Henkel
  • Розуміння того, як роль Enterprise Architect еволюціонує в епоху ШІ

👉 Зареєструватися безкоштовно

Чому це важливо для українського бізнесу?

Autonomous Enterprise відкриває новий рівень конкурентоспроможності: скорочення операційних витрат, прискорення прийняття рішень та відповідність євроінтеграційним стандартам. Українські компанії мають унікальну можливість підготуватися до наступного етапу цифрової трансформації — вже зараз і безкоштовно.

Ера автономного підприємства вже настала. SAP дає всі інструменти, щоб бути серед перших.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Barcelona 2026 - Nov 12-13th
entry.content:
Hi Barcelona and SAP Community!! 🎉

We are thrilled to announce a very special edition of SAP Inside Track Barcelona
This year we celebrate our 10th anniversary! 🎉

A whole decade bringing together the SAP Community in Barcelona — sharing knowledge, building connections, and growing together. And we want to make this milestone bigger and better than ever.

Save the date: November 12th & 13th, 2026

 What is SAP Inside Track?

SAP Inside Track (SIT) is a local, community-driven event where SAP professionals and enthusiasts come together to:

  • Share expertise and experiences
  • Learn from SAP experts and community members
  • Network in an open and informal environment

SIT events are held worldwide and have become a key reference point for the SAP Community.

🤝 Why join us?

Whether you’re a consultant, customer, freelancer, startup, SAP employee — or even new to SAP — this event is for you.

Come to:

  • Learn from inspiring sessions
  • Share your own knowledge
  • Connect with peers and make new friends

The most important thing: your willingness to learn and contribute to the community.

📍 Location

Modernist Room,  Carrer de Roger de Flor 48, Barcelona
https://goo.gl/maps/Kp25w8mZgLo

🗓️ Agenda overview (10th Anniversary Edition)

This year we expand to two days to celebrate properly:

November 12th

  • Masterclass & Opening [afternoon, tbc]

November 13th

  • Full day of additional sessions, networking and special anniversary activities

📌A detailed agenda will be published soon.

🚀 Get involved

Call for Speakers (share your knowledge with the community)  : Coming soon — stay tuned!

Registration: Coming soon — stay tuned!

🌐 Stay connected

🔹SAP Community event page: https://community.sap.com/t5/barcelona-events/sap-inside-track-barcelona-nov-12-13th-2026/ev-p/14404916

We invite you to join Barcelona Group

🔹Website: sitbarcelona.com
🔹Bluesky : sitbarcelona.bsky.social 
🔹Twitter [X] : @sitbarcelona

📢Hashtags: #SITBarcelona #SITBCN

Let’s celebrate 10 years of SAP Inside Track Barcelona together 🥳
We look forward to seeing you all there!

Organizing team:
@Lluis , @ecastellaClinic77 @starverson @saquibus , Mireia Bubí & @david_ruizb

entry.title: Prasad Khandavalli
entry.content:
More about Prasad Khandavalli in SAP Community

 

 


Name: Prasad Khandavalli
Location: Bangalore, India
Occupation: BTP Solution Adoption Advisor at SAP


Prasad, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

I’m PVSB Prasad, part of the SAP BTP Adoption and Consumption team, where I engage with customers globally to drive awareness and accelerate the adoption of SAP BTP technologies. With a background in SAP CX, I focus on extending capabilities across CX and BTP to enable end-to-end, integrated implementations that deliver real business value. I enjoy exploring new technologies, sharing knowledge, and helping teams and customers adopt innovative solutions. Continuous learning, collaboration, and creating meaningful impact through technology are what matter most to me. 

Blog – Read my posts: I actively share insights, learnings, and real-world experiences through my blog contributions in the SAP Community. Outside of work, I enjoy swimming and playing badminton, which help me stay active and maintain a balanced lifestyle. 


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

My area of expertise lies in SAP BTP and SAP CX, with hands-on experience across solutions such as Service Cloud and Sales Cloud V2, SAP C4C, Commerce, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and Qualtrics, along with BTP services like SAP Build Apps, BTP Document Extraction, SAP AI Launchpad, SAP Event Mesh, SAP Translation Hub, and DQM Services. 

I focus on enabling end-to-end, scalable, and intelligent enterprise solutions while driving the adoption of SAP BTP capabilities. This experience allows me to support customers in building integrated and future-ready solutions. A typical day involves architecture discussions, providing technical and strategic advisory, and engaging with global stakeholders to drive successful SAP BTP adoption and consumption. I also dedicate time to mentoring and coaching team members, while actively contributing to the SAP Community through knowledge sharing and collaboration. 


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

The SAP Community played a key role in my early learning journey — through blog posts, answered questions, and real-world insights from practitioners. Contributing back felt like a natural step. Becoming an SAP Champion reflects that ongoing commitment to share knowledge, stay engaged, and support others. To me, it’s not just a recognition — it’s a responsibility to remain active, helpful, and contribute to the growth of the community. 


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

SAP Community is my go-to platform for staying current. I regularly follow blog posts from experts and peers, participate in Q&A discussions, and explore SAP Discovery Center for hands-on learning. One of the most effective ways I learn is by reading community questions—they often highlight real-world challenges that go beyond standard documentation. 

On a regular basis, I answer questions, share insights from my experience, and engage in discussions. I also contribute through webinars, sessions, and content that help others adopt new SAP technologies. 


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

Don’t just consume, contribute. Ask questions, share your learnings, and engage actively. Even simple contributions can create value for others. Follow relevant topics and manage tags to personalize your feed and stay updated. Most importantly, build connections—SAP Community is not just a knowledge platform, but a network of supportive and knowledgeable professionals. 

Prasad’s question to you: What is one practical learning or experience from SAP Community that directly helped you solve a real project challenge?

entry.title: Masashi Otsuka
entry.content:
More about Masashi Otsuka in SAP Community

 

 


Name: Masashi Otsuka
Location: Tokyo, Japan
Occupation: Enterprise Transformation at PWC


Masashi, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

I am an SAP consultant based in Japan, with around 20 years of experience working on SAP projects. Throughout my career, I have been involved in the design, development, and implementation of SAP-based business systems. Currently, I work across multiple SAP projects, supporting technical decision-making and delivery quality, mainly around SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP. What is important to me is not the technology itself, but whether a solution can be used sustainably in real business operations and can be continuously improved over time. I enjoy organizing lessons learned from real projects and sharing them with others, helping teams and projects improve overall quality. 


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

My main areas of expertise are SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, ABAP and ABAP Cloud, and Clean Core extension strategies. I am especially involved in side-by-side development, interface design, and technical quality reviews and standardization. Rather than optimizing a single project, I focus on creating reusable approaches and best practices that can be applied across multiple projects. 

In my daily work, I support multiple SAP projects in parallel, reviewing designs, and discussing technical decisions with project teams. I often work closely with teams to identify quality risks early and to find better design and development approaches together. I am also involved in internal Center of Excellence (CoE) activities, where we identify common challenges across projects and turn project lessons into standards and guidelines. A large part of my work is bringing real project experience back to the CoE and sharing it with other teams. I try to balance supporting individual projects and improving technical quality at an organizational level. 


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

I have been actively involved in SAP community activities in Japan for many years, sharing knowledge from real projects through events, sessions, and information sharing. I wanted to use SAP Community as a way to share those experiences more broadly and to connect with the global community. At the same time, I am interested in bringing global discussions, ideas, and trends back into projects and community activities in Japan. 

During this journey, seeing SAP Champions like Mio Yasutake also became one of the motivations that encouraged me to take this step. As an SAP Champion, I would like to continue contributing as a bridge between local and global communities. 


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

I mainly learn through blog posts, Q&A, and event sessions in SAP Community. I especially focus on practical topics such as Clean Core, SAP BTP, and new SAP policies and directions. In Japan, I am also involved in organizing SAP Inside Track Tokyo, and discussions and insights from these events are an important part of my learning as well. Learning how other practitioners think and make decisions is something I truly value in the community. 

On a weekly basis, I check blog posts and discussions related to topics I am interested in. 
When I find useful insights, I share them internally, reflect them in presentations, or use them in project and community activities. I try to make sure that what I learn does not stay personal, but is shared with teams and the wider community. 


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

My suggestion is to start with real challenges from your own project. Search, read, ask questions, and share what you learn—even small insights can be helpful for others. SAP Community becomes much more valuable when you participate, not just read. As a first step, why not start by simply clicking “Like” on an article you find useful? 
So yes—please give this post a Like, too 😊

entry.title: Simone Azevedo
entry.content:
More about Simone Azevedo in SAP Community

 


Name: Simone Azevedo
Location: Curitiba, Brazil
Occupation:Technical SAP Business Process Analyst at ExxonMobil (via Pantheon Inc) | SAP MM Consultant


Simone, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

I consider myself a highly dedicated person. Whenever I commit to something, I give my very best. Today, I am a mother, a wife, and a professional who truly enjoys working with SAP. Over the past 10 years, my journey with SAP has been both challenging and deeply fulfilling. Along this path, especially after becoming a mother, I learned how important it is to take care of ourselves and seek balance in life. That’swhy I value opportunities that allow me to maintain a good quality of life while continuing to grow professionally. 

I strongly believe that nothing happens by chance. My faith has always guided me through important decisions and challenges, giving me strength and clarity throughout my journey. 


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

My area of expertise is SAP Material Management (MM), supported by a strong background in Supply Chain. This foundation has given me a solid understanding of end-to-end supply chain processes, which hasbeen essential in my transition and growth within SAP MM. 

On a typical day, I support the business by gathering requirements from stakeholders, analyzing their needs, and translating them into effective SAP solutions. I focus on identifying the best approach for each scenario, ensuring process efficiency, system reliability, and alignment with business objectives. 


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

When I first started working with SAP, I deeply admired the SAP Mentors who were always willing to share their knowledge and support others. Their openness and generosity truly inspired me. That inspiration became real when I met @Marssel700 and @FMotter, SAP Mentors and organizers of SAP Inside Track São Paulo. Through their encouragement, I had the idea of bringing the event to Curitiba. They supported me throughout the entire journey, and I am truly grateful for their trust and partnershi 

Today, I am part of the team that organizes several community events, with a special focus on initiatives for women in SAP. This journey has shown me how one person in the SAP Community can inspire othersand create meaningful impact. I want to continue inspiring, engaging, and empowering people — and becoming an SAP Champion would help amplify my voice and give greater visibility to these community efforts. 


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

I learn from the SAP Community almost every day. Community members consistently share valuable experiences and insights that help me stay updated and continuously improve. As an event organizer, I pay close attention to posts that present new ideas, trends, and best practices that we can apply to our local initiatives. I strongly believe that a successful event must provide a great experience for everyone involved — sponsors, attendees, and speakers alike. When the experience is positive for all, people naturally want to participate again. 

Together with the other curators in Curitiba, we work to deliver the best possible experience using the resources available to us. 


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

The key is active participation. Don’t be afraid to ask questions, introduce yourself, or share your thoughts and experiences. Engage with the content, contribute when you can, and attend SAP Community events whenever possible. The more you engage, the more you learn — and the more meaningful connections you build. 

Simone's question to you: When did the SAP Community truly make a difference in your journey?

entry.title: Xavier Le Garrec
entry.content:
More about Xavier Le Garrec in SAP Community

 

Name: Xavier Le Garrec
Location: Lives in San Francisco, USA but originally from Lorient, France 
Occupation: HCM Services North America (Reward to Retain & AI) and Customer Engagement Principal at SAP

 


Xavier, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

My name is Xavier and I have been working as an implementation partner on SAP SuccessFactors modules for about 15 years. What is important to me is empowering customers and other experts to deliver great results in actual projects or in terms of knowledge sharing. At SAP I enjoy networking across business lines to find out and document things that can be useful for all our customers. 


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

I am a subject matter expert for the Compensation Module of SuccessFactors but I am also certified on Performance Management and Employee Central modules. Last year I became a member of the AI Adoption team at SAP which has helped me become knowledgeable on SAP Business AI as well. 

Every morning I open my email inbox and look at my Daily Digest from the community to know how much has been published overnight on topics I follow. If there is content, then I start my day by answering there then move on to customer meetings. When I am not sure about the answer to a question on the community, I log into my demo environment to try to replicate it and provide the best answer possible based on hands-on experimentation. 

I mostly work from home but enjoy riding my bike for 20 minutes to the SAP office once a week. 


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

In 2020 I started writing blog posts because I realized the knowledge gained from specific pain points encountered in implementations was often lost for others to use unless we documented them. Knowledge Base Articles only supplemented implementation guides but didn't help answer critical questions such as leading practices on specific topics or possible workarounds to known limitations. 

In 2025 after writing over 60 blogs and answering thousands of questions on the community I realized I had become the top kudoed author in the Human Capital Management category on the SAP Community. That same year I started following @Martin-Pankraz onLinkedin and saw it said "SAP Champion" on his profile header and looked up the program and thought to myself it would be great to be able to be an active participant in this great team. 


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

I start every day by answering questions raised on the "SAP SuccessFactors Compensation" tag then go on to explore additional tags or blogs.  


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

Save the pages/tags you are interested in in your browser's Favorites so you can look at new content created overnight (blog or help requests) on a daily basis. Tips and Tricks to start writing on the SAP Blog (name of the blogging platform before it was merged with the community) was written by me and I encourage anyone interested in taking part in the community to read it! 

Xavier's question to you: What is the blog of the community that has taught you or helped you the most (your "MVB - most valuable blog")?

entry.title: George Drakos
entry.content:
More about George Drakos in SAP Community

 

 


Name: George Drakos
Location: Athens, Greece
Occupation: SAP Technical Consultant


George, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

I work as an SAP Technical Consultant with over 6.5 years of experience designing and implementing custom SAP solutions, mainly around ABAP, Fiori, ABAP Cloud and clean core principles. My background is in Electrical and Computer Engineering, and during my studies and for a while after I was working on machine learning engineering. I ended up in the SAP world right after finishing my studies. What started as a first step after university turned into something I enjoy doing it every day. 

Outside of work, I spend time at the gym and play electric bass. On the technical side, I really enjoy building things on GitHub and sharing my code, mostly open source tools that are ABAP Cloud compatible. What matters to me the most is helping other developers grow. When I started out I had a lot of help from people around me, and I think SAP is one of those areas where you can't really get far on your own. You don't have easy access to a system to experiment with, the available material is limited, and things move fast with new frameworks like RAP, so it's very easy for someone starting today to feel lost. That's why I try to mentor team members whenever I can and share whatever I've picked up along the way. 


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

My main area of expertise is ABAP Cloud development, with a strong focus on clean core principles and the RAP framework. I work a lot with object-oriented ABAP and design patterns, which in my experience makes a huge difference when projects start to grow. Lately I've been doing quite a bit of work with RAP, building modern Fiori apps end to end on top of it. I'm industry agnostic, so I've had the chance to work across different domains rather than being tied to one specific sector. 

My day usually starts with going through my emails and prioritizing the tasks I have on my plate, since I'm normally working on several projects in parallel. After that, the biggest chunk of my time goes into coding and debugging, which is honestly the part of the job I enjoy the most. The rest of the day is a mix of things: leading the technical design on the projects I'm involved in, doing peer reviews, and tuning programs when something needs to run faster. What really keeps me engaged is when I get a task that actually delivers real value to the users and at the same time pushes me to dig around and figure out the best way to implement it.Those are the ones that keep the job interesting. 


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

For me, being an SAP Champion is essentially a bridge between the technology and the people. You don't have to be the smartest developer in the room who knows every single detail. It's more about being approachable and showing up consistently for others. I wanted to be that person who, when someone asks what they're afraid is a "stupid" question, takes the time to answer properly and makes them feel welcome.Nobody really pushed me towards it. I just wanted to give back to the community the same way people helped me when I was starting out, and the Champion program felt like the right way to do that on a bigger scale. 


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

Apart from the SAP Community itself, I keep a close eye on the latest blog posts and articles being shared on LinkedIn, and I also have 3-4 favourite websites that I check regularly. Putting these sources together givesme a pretty good picture of what's going on in the SAP world at any given moment. My routine starts with checking the latest blog posts in the SAP Community. But for me the process doesn't stop at just reading and understanding the concepts. I always try to implement them hands-on, because I strongly believe that to really get something, you have to apply it yourself. So my daily and weekly rhythm is basically a loop: I find something new in the community, and then I jump straight into the system to try it out. 


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

My biggest suggestion is to not just be a passive reader. Try things out in practice, don't hesitate to ask questions even if you think they might be too simple, and jump into the answers when you can. Engaging in discussions and sharing your own hands-on experiences, both the wins and the roadblocks, is the fastest way to learn and build real connections with other professionals. One more thing, especially for ABAP developers: be open to sharing your code on GitHub. It's something our community has been a bit slow to embrace compared to other tech ecosystems, but it makes a huge difference, both for the people who learn from your repos and for you when you get feedback on your work. 

A question to you, from George:
Is there an SAP feature or framework that you think is underrated, and more developers should be using?

entry.title: Yogananda Muthaiah
entry.content:
More about Yogananda Muthaiah in SAP Community

 

 


Name: Yogananda Muthaiah  
Location: Munich, Germany
Occupation: Senior Technology Consultant at SAP


Yogananda, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

I am Yogananda and I live in Munich, Germany. I’ve been working in SAP for more than 8+yrs as a Senior Technology Consultant. I’m more passionate about new technologies, collaboration, and continuous learning. I enjoy sharing knowledge, mentoring, and building strong connections within the SAP ecosystem. What matters most to me is helping others succeed and contributing to a thriving SAP community. 

I am a technology enthusiast who loves exploring new innovations in enterprise software and preparing for success on customer projects to Go-live. Outside of work, I enjoy problem-solving, engaging with community members, and staying curious about emerging trends (currently on AI). Knowledge-sharing and collaboration are important to me because they empower individuals and strengthen the SAP Community. 


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

My expertise is currently in various product areas (SAP BTP, SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Integration Suite, CAP, SAP UI5, SAP CPQ, SAP Incentive Management. etc)  and enterprise solutions, with a focus on customer implementation, design & development, and guiding customers through their SAP transformation journeys. I specialize in helping customers leverage SAP tools effectively to drive business outcomes. 

My typical day involves collaborating with internal colleagues, customers, and sometimes with Partners solving technical challenges and working on SAP projects. I spend time researching new features, engaging with community content, and applying innovative solutions to real-world business problems. 


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

I was inspired by the spirit of collaboration and knowledge exchange in the SAP Community. Seeing how impactful shared experiences and mentorship can motivate me to contribute actively and help others grow in their SAP careers. 


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

II learn by reading blogs, Q&A discussions, and tutorials, attending webinars, and engaging in conversations with experts. The diversity of perspectives in the community helps me stay updated and continuously improve my skills. 

Daily: Checking questions, answering queries, and reading blogs.
Weekly: Participating in discussions, contributing insights, and sharing my own experiences through blogs or comments.
Regularly: Networking with peers and exploring new SAP topics to stay ahead. 


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

My key advice is simple — engage actively. Ask questions, share your experiences, and contribute meaningfully to discussions. The more consistent you participate, the more you learn, grow, and build valuable relationships. Consistency, curiosity, and a collaborative mindset are the cornerstones of making the most of what the SAP Community has to offer. 

Yogananda's question to you: What is one SAP Community connection or collaboration that has had the biggest impact on your professional journey — and what made it so significant?

entry.title: Dmitry Kuznetsov
entry.content:
More about Dmitry Kuznetsov in SAP Community

 

 

Name: Dmitry Kuznetsov
Location: Netherlands 
Occupation: SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics consultant


Dmitry, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

I am an SAP S/4HANA Embedded Analytics consultant, trainer, and certified Databricks data engineer. With 20 years in SAP, I worked across every major data shift—from BW to HANA to Datasphere. I am a lifelong learner, fortunate to have grown alongside SAP’s many technology shifts. I enjoy sharpening my instincts and diving into new challenges—figuratively in tech, and through sailing and kitesurfing.


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

I specialize in Data & Analytics within the SAP ecosystem. My core expertise includes BW and HANA modeling, along with hands-on work in ABAP CDS and Fiori-based reporting. Recently, I have expanded into Databricks, helping customers migrate and modernize their data platforms.  

My day-to-day work spans consulting and architecture: designing solutions, solving data challenges, and guiding implementations. Alongside this, I teach for SAP Education and regularly speak at conferences of all sizes.


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

I have been an active member of the SAP Community for some time and have connected with many SAP Champions along the way. The turning point came at in-person TechEd Berlin 2025, where meeting face-to-face made me realize it was time to step forward and be nominated. I am grateful for the support I received and proud of this recognition. 


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

I learn in the SAP Community by actively engaging: reading blogs, following discussions, and, importantly, answering questions. For me, the community goes beyond SAP-managed platforms. It extends to SIT events and partner-organized SAP insider conferences, where I have been connecting with peers and exchanging ideas for over a decade.


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

Do not limit yourself to online interaction. Seek out local grassroots events—they offer a completely different level of engagement and connection.

Dmitry's question to you:
Why do you need all this data?

entry.title: Adarsh Rao
entry.content:
More about Adarsh Rao in SAP Community

 


Name: Adarsh Rao
Location: Düsseldorf, Germany
Occupation: Enterprise Integrations and Architecture at SAP


Adarsh, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

My name is Adarsh Rao, and I work as a SAP Integration Consultant. My role mainly involves building/ advising customers on connecting different systems and ensuring data flows smoothly across them. I especially enjoy working on complex integration scenarios where there is no straightforward solution. I like learning new things, experimenting with ideas, and sharing practical solutions based on real project experience. 

I enjoy understanding problems deeply and finding practical solutions. I like working on challenges that require a bit of thinking beyond standard approaches. What is important to me is continuous learning, doing quality work, and sharing knowledge so that others can benefit from it as well. 


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

My main area of expertise is SAP Integration (SAP Integration Suite, SAP PI/PO, SAP XI). I focus on designing, developing, and supporting complex integration scenarios between SAP and non-SAP systems. 
A typical day includes analyzing integration requirements, designing solutions, resolving issues that are not straightforward, and supporting ongoing integrations. I also spend time collaborating with my project team members, documenting solutions, and learning about new SAP features and best practices. 


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

While working on complex problems, I often found valuable ideas and solutions shared by others in SAP Community. This inspired me to become an SAP Champion so I could also share my own learnings and help others who face similar non‑standard challenges. 


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

I learn mainly by reading blog posts, exploring different solution approaches, and following experienced community members. I especially learn a lot from posts that explain how real problems were solved in real projects. I regularly follow integration-related content and read new blog posts. Whenever I encounter a problem with no direct solution, I try to share my ideas or approaches based on experience. Weekly, I spend some time actively engaging with content and contributing when I feel my input can help others. 


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

Be curious, be active, and don’t hesitate to share your ideas even if the solution is not perfect. SAP Community becomes most valuable when people share how they solved complex or uncommon problems. 

Adarsh's question to you: What was the most complex problem you solved with the help of SAP Community, and what did you learn from it?

entry.title: Martin Jonen
entry.content:
More about Martin Jonen in LinkedIn






Name: Martin Jonen
Location: Lucerne, Switzerland
Occupation: SAP ABAP Software Engineer


Martin, tell us about yourself.
Who are you, what do you like to do, what is important to you?

My name is Martin Jonen, and I live in Lucerne, Switzerland. I am a freelance SAP Software Engineer, coach, and lifelong learner. I've been working with SAP technologies for more than 15 years, primarily in the insurance industry, helping teams build reliable and maintainable software solutions. What I enjoy most is learning, solving complex problems, and helping others grow. Whether it's improving code quality, teaching Test-Driven Development, or supporting developers in their professional journey, I love seeing people gain confidence and achieve things they didn't think were possible. What's important to me is continuous improvement. Not only as a developer but also as a human being. I believe great software is built by people who take care of both their technical skills and their well-being.


What is your area of expertise?
What does a usual day at work look like for you?

My main areas of expertise are ABAP development, Clean Code, Test-Driven Development (TDD), automated testing, and software design. I am passionate about helping SAP developers move beyond simply making code work and towards building software that is maintainable, testable, and easy to understand. I also enjoy coaching developers and teams on modern engineering practices and helping them become more effective and confident in their daily work.

A typical day includes a mix of software development, architecture discussions, code reviews, coaching, and learning. I currently work on SAP solutions in the insurance sector, where I help improve business processes, increase software quality, and support modernization initiatives. Part of my day is often dedicated to mentoring developers, creating educational content, preparing training material, or experimenting with new technologies and development approaches. I believe that staying hands-on is one of the best ways to keep learning.


What inspired you to become an SAP Champion in SAP Community?

The SAP Community has played a significant role throughout my career. I've learned a lot from blogs, discussions, conference talks, and the willingness of community members to share their knowledge. Being nominated as an SAP Champion was a great honor because it represents the spirit of giving back. I enjoy sharing what I learn, helping others avoid common mistakes, and contributing to a community that has helped me grow for many years.


How do you learn in SAP Community?
What is your daily/weekly routine in SAP Community?

I learn by reading blog posts, attending community events, participating in discussions, and most importantly, by experimenting. Whenever I discover a new concept or technology, I like to apply it in a practical setting to understand its strengths and limitations. I also learn a lot through conversations with other community members. The diversity of experiences and perspectives within the SAP Community is really valuable.

I regularly read SAP Community blogs, follow discussions on LinkedIn and other channels, and participate in SAP-related events whenever possible. I also contribute by sharing content around ABAP, Clean Code, Test-Driven Development, and software craftsmanship. Through blog posts, conference talks, community events, and coaching, I try to help developers build better software and enjoy their work more.


What is your suggestion to get the most out of SAP Community?

Be curious and contribute before you feel ready. Many people think they need to be experts before sharing their knowledge. In my experience, some of the most valuable contributions come from people who have just solved a problem and are willing to share what they learned. Ask questions, join discussions, attend events, and share your experiences. The more you engage with the community, the more you will learn and the more opportunities will come your way.

Martin's question to you: What has been the most valuable connection, opportunity, or friendship you've gained through the SAP Community?
